2606.13562.title: Contrast-Informed Augmentation and Domain-Adversarial Training for Adult-to-Neonatal MR Reconstruction Generalization
2606.13562.paper_summary: Purpose: To investigate whether contrast-informed data augmentation and domain-adversarial training improve the adult-to-neonatal generalization of the E2E-VarNet. Methods: Three training regimes were investigated: (1) adult-only training with unaugmented adult data, (2) mixed training with paired unaugmented and neonatal-informed augmented adult data, and (3) mixed training with a domain-adversarial objective. Models were trained on retrospectively undersampled multi-coil adult T2-weighted brain MR data and evaluated on neonatal and adult test data at acceleration factors $R=4$ and $R=8$ using quantitative metrics and qualitative evaluation. Feature analyses assessed whether domain-adversarial training altered the latent representations of unaugmented adult, augmented adult, and neonatal test samples. Results: Mixed training (Mixed) and mixed domain-adversarial training (Mixed-DAT) outperformed unaugmented adult-only training (Unaug-Only) when evaluated on neonatal data. At R=4, Mixed-DAT achieved the best performance (SSIM = 0.924 +/- 0.027, PSNR = 33.98 +/- 1.15 dB). At R=8, Mixed-DAT performed best when measured using SSIM (0.848 +/- 0.031 vs. 0.766 +/- 0.037 for Unaug-Only and 0.814 +/- 0.035 for Mixed) and Mixed performed best when measured using PSNR (29.56 +/- 0.83 dB vs. 26.26 +/- 0.78 dB for Unaug-Only and 29.43 +/- 0.83 dB for Mixed-DAT). Qualitative assessment of t-SNE plots suggested that Mixed-DAT increased the overlap among the latent representations of the unaugmented adult, augmented adult, and neonatal test data. Conclusion: Contrast-informed augmentation and domain-adversarial training improved adult-to-neonatal generalization of deep learning-based MR reconstruction. These findings suggest that contrast-informed data augmentation combined with adversarial training may improve robustness to domain shift in undersampled neonatal MR reconstruction.
2606.13562.paper_summary_zh: 目的：調查對比知識增強的數據增強和領域對抗訓練是否改善E2E-VarNet的成人到新生兒的泛化能力。<lb/>方法：研究了三種訓練方案：（1）僅使用未增強的成人數據進行成人專用訓練，（2）混合訓練，使用配對的未增強和新生兒知識增強的成人數據，以及（3）具有領域對抗目標的混合訓練。<lb/>模型在回顧性欠採樣的多線圈成人T2加權腦部MR數據上進行訓練，並在加速因子$R=4$和$R=8$下，使用定量指標和定性評估對新生兒和成人測試數據進行評估。<lb/>特徵分析評估了領域對抗訓練是否改變了未增強成人、增強成人和新生兒測試樣本的潛在表示。<lb/>結果：在對新生兒數據進行評估時，混合訓練（Mixed）和混合領域對抗訓練（Mixed-DAT）優於未增強的成人專用訓練（Unaug-Only）。<lb/>在R=4時，Mixed-DAT達到了最佳性能（SSIM = 0.924 +/- 0.027，PSNR = 33.98 +/- 1.15 dB）。<lb/>在R=8時，Mixed-DAT在使用SSIM測量時表現最佳（0.848 +/- 0.031對比Unaug-Only的0.766 +/- 0.037和Mixed的0.814 +/- 0.035），而Mixed在使用PSNR測量時表現最佳（29.56 +/- 0.83 dB對比Unaug-Only的26.26 +/- 0.78 dB和Mixed-DAT的29.43 +/- 0.83 dB）。<lb/>對t-SNE圖的定性評估表明，Mixed-DAT增加了未增強成人、增強成人和新生兒測試數據的潛在表示之間的重疊。<lb/>結論：對比知識增強的增強和領域對抗訓練改善了基於深度學習的MR重建的成人到新生兒的泛化能力。<lb/>這些發現表明，對比知識增強的數據增強結合對抗訓練可能改善對欠採樣新生兒MR重建中的領域轉移的魯棒性。
2606.13562.author: Stephen Moore et.al.
2606.13562.authors: Stephen Moore, Lara Leijser, Richard Frayne, Roberto Souza
